questions.text: A 2D skeleton outline that can be folded to make a 3D shape is called a:
questions.explanation: A net is a 2-dimensional shape that can be folded to form a 3-dimensional object.

questions.text: How many faces does a cube have?
questions.explanation: A cube is a solid figure with 6 equal square faces.

questions.text: The points where the edges of a solid meet are called:
questions.explanation: Vertices are the corner points of a 3D shape.
A sphere is perfectly round and has no flat faces, edges, or vertices.

questions.text: How many edges does a triangular pyramid have?
questions.explanation: A triangular pyramid (tetrahedron) has 3 edges on the base and 3 rising to the apex.

questions.text: An isometric sketch shows the ________ of the object.
questions.explanation: Isometric sketches use dot paper to show 3D objects with proportional dimensions.

questions.text: A cross-section of a cylinder cut horizontally is a:
questions.explanation: Cutting a cylinder parallel to its base results in a circular cross-section.

questions.text: How many vertices does a cuboid have?
Which of the following is a 3D object?
questions.explanation: A cone has depth and occupies space, making it a 3D (three-dimensional) shape.
A cylinder has 2 circular flat faces and 1 curved surface.
